description:
Represents a serverless function that executes code in response to triggers or direct invocations.
Functions are the primary compute resource in serverless applications, designed to be stateless and ephemeral.

arcEnabled.description:
Whether the function can be invoked via ARC (Alien Remote Call) protocol from the control plane.
When enabled, the necessary queue infrastructure is automatically created for the target platform.

code.description: Code for the function, either a pre-built image or source code to be built.

src.description: The source directory to build from

binaryName.description: Name of the binary to build and run

oneOf.description: TypeScript/JavaScript compiled to single executable with Bun

binaryName.description: Name of the compiled binary (defaults to package.json name if not specified)

concurrencyLimit.description:
Maximum number of concurrent executions allowed for the function.
None means platform default applies.

environment.description: Key-value pairs to set as environment variables for the function.

id.description:
Identifier for the function. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

oneOf.description: Controls network accessibility of the function.

items.description:
New ResourceRef that works with any resource type.
This can eventually replace the enum-based ResourceRef for full extensibility.

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

links.description: List of resource references this function depends on.

memoryMb.description:
Memory allocated to the function in megabytes (MB).
Constraints: 128‑32768 MB (platform-specific limits may apply)
Default: 256

permissions.description:
Permission profile name that defines the permissions granted to this function.
This references a profile defined in the stack's permission definitions.

oneOf.description:
Optional readiness probe configuration.
Only applicable for functions with Public ingress.
When configured, the probe will be executed after provisioning/update to verify the function is ready.

method.description:
HTTP method to use for the probe request.
Default: GET

path.description:
Path to request for the probe (e.g., "/health", "/ready").
Default: "/"

timeoutSeconds.description:
Maximum execution time for the function in seconds.
Constraints: 1‑3600 seconds (platform-specific limits may apply)
Default: 30

oneOf.description: Function triggered by queue messages (always 1 message per invocation)

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

oneOf.description: Function triggered by storage events (future implementation)

type.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

oneOf.description: Function triggered by scheduled events (future implementation)

items.description: Defines what triggers a function execution

triggers.description:
List of triggers that define what events automatically invoke this function.
If empty, the function is only invokable directly via HTTP calls or platform-specific invocation APIs.
When configured, the function will be automatically invoked when any of the specified trigger conditions are met.
